name: epic-tech-ai-desktop
description: Native desktop wrapper for Epic Tech AI. Boots the local Python daemon and Next.js dashboard in a native window.
author: Epic Tech AI <epictechai@gmail.com>

_signing_comments.publisherName: SET THIS to the EXACT subject Common Name (CN) of your code-signing certificate, e.g. 'RealDealCPA LLC' (Azure Trusted Signing) or 'Valentino <Lastname>' (individual OV cert). It is currently a PLACEHOLDER — while it does not match a real signed binary, keep build.win.verifyUpdateCodeSignature = false (see below). See docs/SIGNING.md.
_signing_comments.sign: build.win.sign points at ./sign.js — a custom electron-builder code-signing hook. It signs each artifact ONLY when signing credentials are present in the environment (Azure Trusted Signing or signtool/eSigner); with no creds it logs and returns so the build proceeds UNSIGNED instead of failing. Secrets are passed through by .github/workflows/release.yml.
_signing_comments.verifyUpdateCodeSignature: Kept FALSE on purpose: electron-updater would otherwise reject every unsigned self-update because the downloaded installer's signature would not match the placeholder publisherName above. Once you configure REAL signing (creds + a publisherName that matches the cert subject) and ship one signed release, flip this to true to turn the auto-updater into a genuine chain of trust. CAVEAT: the FIRST signed release must still be installed over an unsigned one (that transition update is not signature-verified); every release after that must stay signed with the same publisher or installed apps refuse the update.

win.publisherName: RealDealCPA — REPLACE with the exact cert subject CN
